title: Aircraft and Helicopters (6th-Gen, Apache, LCA Mark-1A, Rafale M)
focus: Key features, generations, and capabilities of recently highlighted fighter jets and attack helicopters.
rationale: Covers multiple aircraft with distinct features (AESA radar, omnirole, 4.5 vs 6th gen) which are prime candidates for match-the-following or statement-based questions.
sectionExcerpt: Sixth-generation fighter jets are characterized by attributes such as AI integration, Hypersonic capabilities... Apache AH-64E: Multi-role attack helicopter... LCA Mark-1A: 4.5 generation, all weather and multi-role fighter aircraft... Rafale Marine (M): Omnirole capabilities... 4.5 generation... deployed on INS Vikrant.
evidenceBullets: 6th-Gen fighters feature AI integration, hypersonic, and unmanned capabilities (e.g., China's J-36 and J-50).
Apache AH-64E is a multi-role attack helicopter with Hellfire missiles and advanced sensors.
LCA Mark-1A is a 4.5 generation fighter equipped with AESA Radar and an EW suite.
Rafale Marine (M) is a 4.5 generation omnirole fighter by Dassault Aviation, to be deployed on INS Vikrant.

title: Naval Ships: INS Arnala and INS Nistar
focus: Indigenous naval vessels, their specific classes (ASW-SWC, Diving Support), and primary functions.
rationale: Distinct ship classes with specific indigenous milestones (first ASW-SWC, first Diving Support Vessel) that are highly testable.
sectionExcerpt: INS Arnala: India's first Anti-Submarine Warfare Shallow Water Craft (ASW-SWC). Largest Indian Naval warship to be propelled by a Diesel Engine-Waterjet combination. INS Nistar: First indigenously designed and constructed Diving Support Vessel... Will serve as 'Mother Ship' for the Deep Submergence Rescue Vessel (DSRV).
evidenceBullets: INS Arnala is India's first Anti-Submarine Warfare Shallow Water Craft (ASW-SWC).
INS Arnala uses a Diesel Engine-Waterjet combination for propulsion.
INS Nistar is India's first indigenously designed Diving Support Vessel.
INS Nistar serves as a 'Mother Ship' for Deep Submergence Rescue Vessels (DSRV) for deep sea saturation diving up to 300m.

title: UAVs and Anti-Drone Systems
focus: Emerging drone technologies including hybrid VTOL UAVs, hard-kill anti-drone systems, and EW-resistant fiber optic drones.
rationale: Groups all drone-related technologies. The contrast between offensive UAVs, defensive anti-drone systems, and EW-resistant drones provides excellent material for conceptual questions.
sectionExcerpt: Rudrastra: A Hybrid Vertical Take-Off and Landing (VTOL) UAV... Equipped with precision-guided anti-personnel warheads. Bhargavastra: A low-cost, 'hard kill' anti-drone system designed to neutralize threats from drone swarms... Two-layer security system. Fiber Optic Drones: Operated using long fiber optic cables instead of radio links... No radio link means they cannot be jammed or intercepted by EW systems.
evidenceBullets: Rudrastra is a Hybrid VTOL UAV equipped with precision-guided anti-personnel warheads.
Bhargavastra is an indigenous 'hard kill' anti-drone system with a two-layer security system (unguided micro-rockets and guided micro-missiles).
Fiber optic drones use glass tube cables instead of radio links, making them immune to electronic warfare (EW) jamming.
Fiber optic cables allow faster speeds, longer distances, and simultaneous communications on different wavelengths.

title: Long-Range Glide Bomb 'Gaurav'
focus: Characteristics, navigation scheme, and range of the DRDO-developed Gaurav glide bomb.
rationale: A standalone indigenous weapons system with specific weight, range, and navigation parameters.
sectionExcerpt: DRDO successfully conducts release trials of indigenously developed Long-Range Glide Bomb 'Gaurav'. Glide bomb after launch steer towards the target using highly accurate hybrid navigation scheme with a combination of Indian Navigation System (INS) and Global Positioning System (GPS) data. Weight: 1,000 kg class. Range: 'Gaurav' achieved 100 km range with pinpoint accuracy.
evidenceBullets: 'Gaurav' is an indigenously developed Long-Range Glide Bomb by DRDO.
It uses a hybrid navigation scheme combining Indian Navigation System (INS) and GPS data.
It belongs to the 1,000 kg weight class and has a strike range of 100 km.

title: Project 17A Stealth Frigates
focus: Details of Project 17A, including the commissioned ships, builders, and propulsion systems.
rationale: Major indigenous naval shipbuilding project. Details like CODOG propulsion and builders (Mazagon Dock, GRSE) are standard UPSC prelims facts.
sectionExcerpt: Two multi-mission stealth frigates of Project 17A - INS Udaygiri and INS Himgiri, were commissioned into the Indian Navy. Follow-on ships of Shivalik-class (Project 17) frigates. Under it, Seven Frigates being indigenously built by Mazagon Dock (Mumbai) and GRSE (Kolkata). Configured with Combined Diesel or Gas (CODOG) main propulsion plants.
evidenceBullets: Project 17A involves the construction of seven multi-mission stealth frigates, acting as follow-ons to the Shivalik-class.
INS Udaygiri and INS Himgiri are newly commissioned frigates under Project 17A.
The frigates are indigenously built by Mazagon Dock (Mumbai) and GRSE (Kolkata).
They utilize Combined Diesel or Gas (CODOG) main propulsion plants.

title: Netra MkII Airborne Early Warning and Control System
focus: Features and platform of the next-generation Netra MkII AWACS programme.
rationale: Important airborne surveillance system upgrade. The specific platform (Airbus A321) and its function as a flying command centre are key testable points.
sectionExcerpt: The government has given a go-ahead to the production of next generation airborne early warning and control system (AWACS) India programme also referred to as the Netra MkII. It will be mounted on the Airbus A321 platform. Key Features: Detect, track, and monitor threats across vast distances. Much more than an aircraft as it is a flying command centre.
evidenceBullets: Netra MkII is India's next-generation Airborne Early Warning and Control System (AWACS) programme.
The Netra MkII system will be mounted on the Airbus A321 platform.
It functions as a flying command centre to detect, track, and monitor threats over vast distances.
